operating_state: Total production increased 52 MBOED to 1,806 MBOED driven by new wells in Lower 48 unconventional plays and development programs across Alaska, Australia, Canada, China and Malaysia. Average realized price decreased to $60.05 per BOE from $83.07 per BOE due to lower commodity prices with Brent crude averaging $86.76 per barrel versus $100.85 per barrel and Henry Hub natural gas averaging $2.54 per MMBTU versus $8.20 per MMBTU.
cost_structure: Production and operating expenses increased due to higher well work activity and production volumes in Lower 48. DD&A expenses increased $223 million driven by higher rates from reserve revisions caused by higher operating costs and lower prices, plus higher production volumes. Purchased commodities decreased $3.7 billion due to lower prices across all commodities and reduced volumes.
material_events: Completed acquisition of remaining 50% Surmont interest for approximately $2.7 billion in October 2023, funded by debt issuance comprising senior notes due 2033, 2054, and 2063. Declared 14% quarterly dividend increase from $0.51 to $0.58 per share effective Q1 2024. Signed 15-year throughput agreement for regasification capacity at Gate LNG terminal in Netherlands beginning 2031.
macro_exposures: Company remains unhedged and exposed to cyclical commodity price volatility, monitoring market fundamentals including Middle East conflicts, OPEC Plus supplies, global demand, inventory levels, governmental policies, inflation and supply chain disruptions. Foreign currency transaction gains decreased due to lower USD strengthening against NOK and losses on forward contracts related to Surmont acquisition.
forward_outlook: Q4 2023 production expected at 1.86-1.90 MMBOED with full-year guidance of approximately 1.82 MMBOED versus prior 1.80-1.81 MMBOED due to Surmont acquisition. Full-year DD&A guidance updated to $8.3 billion from $8.2 billion. Reconfirmed 2023 planned return of capital to shareholders of $11 billion through three-tier framework, significantly exceeding commitment to return greater than 30% of anticipated operating cash flows.
uncertainty_profile: Subject to lawsuits involving oil and gas royalty payments, gas measurement methods, contract disputes, environmental damages and climate change with primary exposures relating to alleged royalty underpayments and environmental contamination from historic operations. Environmental accrual totaled $187 million at September 30, 2023. Forward-looking statements acknowledge material risks from commodity price fluctuations, regulatory changes addressing climate concerns, and potential operational disruptions from geopolitical events.
